title: frangula sphaerosperma
intro: Frangula sphaerosperma, also known as Buckthorn or Alder Buckthorn, is a deciduous shrub native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its dark green leaves and small yellow flowers. The plant contains anthraquinones, a type of compound with medicinal properties that have been used to treat various gastrointestinal issues. Frangula sphaerosperma has been traditionally used to treat constipation, diarrhea, and other digestive problems due to its ability to stimulate bowel movements and improve gut health. Herbal preparations of Frangula sphaerosperma include teas, tinctures, and capsules, which can be used under the guidance of a healthcare professional to support overall digestive health.
